summary: Ukrainian forces struck the Sevastopol and Kerch ports, causing fires and a massive attack on the sea port. Drone activity was reported across multiple Ukrainian regions, including Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Zaporizhzhia. Power and water outages were imposed in occupied Crimea following drone attacks on infrastructure.
key_points: Ukrainian attacks set fire to the Sevastopol sea port and caused additional fires at the Kerch port and a military unit 98546 (Source: @UkraineNow/82086).
Occupied Crimea experienced partial blackouts in north‑west, central and south‑coastal energy zones after drone strikes on Russian infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).

Multiple UAVs were observed heading toward Bogoduhiv, Chuhuiv, Vysokopillya, and other locations in Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Zaporizhzhia, and central regions (Source: @kpszsu/65928; @kpszsu/65904; @kpszsu/65871).
A ballistic missile threat was noted from the south, indicating possible launches from Crimea (Source: @kpszsu/65941).
Ukrainian forces reported taking the village of Slavyansk and advancing on Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka in Donbass (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
Russian air defenses were overloaded by UAV swarms, leading to demonstrative strikes on Moscow refineries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
A curfew and transport restrictions were imposed in Sevastopol by occupying authorities (Source: @UkraineNow/82102).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces launched a massive attack on the Sevastopol sea port, igniting fires and striking the nearby Kerch port and a military unit. Drone strikes across occupied Crimea caused widespread power outages and water supply issues. Multiple UAV sightings across Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Zaporizhzhia indicate heightened aerial activity on several fronts.

points: Ukrainian forces reported setting fire to the Sevastopol sea port and causing additional fires at the Kerch port and military unit 98546, highlighting a significant maritime strike (Source: @UkraineNow/82086).

Ukrainian troops claimed capture of the village of Slavyansk and ongoing assaults on Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka in Donbass, indicating offensive momentum in the east (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
Ukrainian air defenses reported being overwhelmed by UAV swarms, prompting Russian strikes on Moscow refineries, underscoring the intensity of aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
Ukrainian command noted increased demand for fuel due to continuous attacks on Russian refineries, affecting logistics on the southern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
Ukrainian forces observed UAVs heading toward Bogoduhiv and Chuhuiv in northern Kharkiv, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions in that sector (Source: @kpszsu/65928).
Russian‑occupied Crimea experienced partial blackouts in north‑west, central and south‑coastal energy zones after drone attacks on critical infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Residents of occupied Crimea reported water supply problems following the same infrastructure attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
A curfew was imposed in Sevastopol, limiting public transport and closing major retail venues, reflecting heightened security measures (Source: @UkraineNow/82102).
Russian authorities noted a ballistic missile threat emanating from the south, likely linked to Crimea (Source: @kpszsu/65941).
Russian air defense systems were reported to be overloaded by UAVs, leading to demonstrative strikes on Moscow refineries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
UAVs were observed over Kharkiv heading toward Bogoduhiv and Chuhuiv, indicating active aerial operations in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65928).
UAV activity was reported from the north of Kharkiv, suggesting continued reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/65871).
Ukrainian forces claimed advances in Donbass, taking Slavyansk and pressing attacks on Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
UAVs were sighted over Sumy and Kharkiv from the north, highlighting persistent aerial pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/65918).
A reactive UAV was reported near Vysokopillya in Kherson region, showing cross‑front UAV deployment (Source: @kpszsu/65904).
UAVs were reported over Chernihiv heading toward Chernihiv, Ponornitsia, and Bahmach, indicating northern aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/65871).
UAVs from northern Zhytomyr were observed heading toward Horodnytsia, showing pressure on the north‑west axis (Source: @kpszsu/65871).
UAVs were seen over Rivne region from northern Volyn, targeting Goshcha and Rivne (Source: @kpszsu/65871).
UAVs were reported near Kyiv from the north, with a specific mention of a route via Zhytomyr/Makariv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67951).
A UAV incident was noted near Starokostiantyniv, indicating activity in the north‑west (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67970).
UAVs were observed heading north‑west from the Kerch area, suggesting a ballistic threat from Crimea toward southern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/65941).
Ukrainian forces reported a ballistic strike threat over Odesa region, linked to Crimea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67981).
UAVs were sighted over Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions, indicating continued southern aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/65948).
UAV groups were reported launching from Primorsko-Akhtarsk and Bryansk districts, showing southern and western launch points (Source: @eRadarrua/97675).
Ukrainian authorities noted increased fuel demand due to attacks on Russian refineries affecting southern logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
Russian air defenses were overwhelmed by a swarm of UAVs, leading to demonstrative strikes on Moscow refineries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
UAV activity was reported across multiple fronts, including Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Zaporizhzhia, and the north‑west, indicating a broad aerial contest (Source: @kpszsu/65871).
A pair of Tu‑95MS bombers were relocated from Olenya airfield to the Far East, suggesting strategic bomber redeployment (Source: @eRadarrua/97665).
UAV launch groups were identified from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and Bryansk regions, highlighting Russian use of multiple launch bases (Source: @eRadarrua/97675).
A helicopter Robinson R44 disappeared in Primorsky Krai, unrelated to the Ukraine conflict and thus omitted from operational analysis.

key_developments: Ukrainian forces ignited fires at Sevastopol sea port and Kerch port, damaging Russian maritime infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82086).
Drone strikes caused partial blackouts and water supply issues in occupied Crimea, disrupting Russian civilian services (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
UAV sightings across Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Zaporizhzhia, and northern regions indicate intensified aerial operations on multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/65871).
Russian air defenses were saturated by UAV swarms, prompting retaliatory strikes on Moscow refineries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
Ukrainian troops reported capturing Slavyansk and advancing on Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka in Donbass (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31054).
A ballistic missile threat from the south, likely originating in Crimea, was highlighted, raising concerns for southern Ukrainian regions (Source: @kpszsu/65941).
Curfew and transport restrictions imposed in Sevastopol reflect heightened Russian security measures in occupied territories (Source: @UkraineNow/82102).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian-occupied Crimea faces infrastructure degradation due to Ukrainian drone attacks, reducing energy and water availability for both military and civilian populations. The proliferation of UAVs across all fronts strains Russian air defenses, forcing reactive strikes on strategic targets like Moscow refineries. Ballistic missile threats from the south suggest possible escalation of long‑range attacks against Ukrainian southern regions, while Ukrainian advances in Donbass increase pressure on Russian defensive lines.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command appears to be leveraging UAVs and targeted strikes to disrupt Russian logistical hubs and weaken occupied territories. Russian authorities are responding with curfews, transport shutdowns, and heightened air defense alerts, indicating a defensive posture aimed at protecting critical infrastructure. Both sides are intensifying aerial operations, reflecting a shift toward a more contested airspace across the theater.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV launch sites in Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and Bryansk is essential for anticipating future strike patterns. The blackout and water disruptions in Crimea may affect Russian troop sustainment and civilian morale, presenting opportunities for further pressure. The reported ballistic threat from Crimea warrants heightened alertness for southern Ukrainian air defense units.
